Indicative Acquisition Term Sheet (India) — Velorum Nexus Private Limited (Acquirer) and Sunhollow Retail Limited (Target, listed on Metabridge Exchange: MBX).
Structure: Acquisition of 61% of the paid-up equity share capital via Share Purchase Agreement and mandatory open offer pursuant to the Indian Securities Regulator’s Takeover Regulations.
Consideration and financing: INR 412 per share payable in cash; funded by Velorum internal accruals and a bridge facility from Kalypsis Credit Opportunities Fund I, without any security interest over personal data.
Conditions precedent: ISR disclosure filings, MBX intimation, Board approvals, Competition Authority of Bharat clearance, and notification to the Data Governance Board of Bharat if cross-border processing thresholds are met.
UPSI/MNPI: Unpublished price sensitive information will be handled under Target’s Insider Trading Code aligned with PIT Regulations; clean team-only access; trading window remains closed until ISR-permitted public disclosure; no UPSI shared beyond designated insiders.
Data protection framework: Data Processing Addendum (DPA) Schedule 2 is appended to govern diligence data; purpose limitation and data minimisation apply; no Aadhaar, PAN, or GSTIN will be collected for deal diligence; legacy copies (if any) were erased on 2026-05-01 with erasure certificates attached.
DSAR and consent withdrawal: Each party will maintain a DSAR portal and a consent withdrawal channel for any individual data inadvertently processed; SLA of 7 days for access and 30 days for erasure, with audit logs retained for 1 year.
Onboarding forms: Revised vendor/investor onboarding forms explicitly exclude Aadhaar/PAN fields; child (under 18) onboarding is not applicable to the transaction and guardian-consent templates exist but are not in active use.
Cross-border transfer: Post-closing, limited HR and investor contact metadata may be transferred to processors in the Lumeria Republic under Data Export Approval DEX-2048; approval is already granted and no live transfer is pending before closing.
Retention and deletion: DPA Schedule 3 sets 90-day retention for pre-closing diligence workpapers and 5-year retention for executed contracts; destruction certificates and deletion attestations will be exchanged within 15 days of closing.
Security and compliance memos: Each party represents implementation of reasonable security practices per Indian law; privacy compliance memos dated 2026-05-15 are appended; this clause does not require collection of any new identifiers.
MNPI controls: Target will maintain an insider list and window management; Acquirer will enforce clean-room access; nothing herein authorises sharing of UPSI on public channels; for clarity, no MNPI is being released by this term sheet.
DPIA and policy updates: Within 30 days post-closing, parties will complete a joint DPIA under the DPDPA and update Insider Trading Codes; any minimisation gaps identified have already been remediated per the attached closure memo.
Third-party consents: ISR observation letter, MBX acknowledgement of disclosure, CAB clearance, and lender NOCs (if any) are conditions to closing; no consent from any authority for government ID databases is required.
Employee/contractor data: Any processing of employee or contractor PII for integration will use pseudonymised datasets; no active collection of sensitive identifiers is contemplated, and back-up tapes will be logically segregated prior to migration.
Notices and contacts: Primary contact—Ms. Rhea Kannan, Company Secretary, Sunhollow Retail Limited, rhea.kannan@sunhollow.example.in; Acquirer contact—Mr. Dev Malhotra, VP Corporate Development, dev.malhotra@velorumnexus.example.com.
Governing law and confidentiality: Governing law—laws of India; exclusive jurisdiction—courts of Shantipur, Vardha Pradesh; confidentiality per existing NDA; this document is non-binding except for confidentiality, exclusivity (30 days), and governing law.
